rationale: In the citing paper, MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) is cited multiple times in the core argument, not just as a passing background reference. It is used to support key framing claims about low-involvement processing and the role of creative/emotional versus informational content, and it is referenced again when motivating controls and interpreting results. This shows meaningful conceptual and interpretive dependence. The citing article then pushes that same line of inquiry with finer-grained cue coding and a new modeling approach.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) as prior evidence that ad content moderates advertising effectiveness in established FMCG contexts. It extends that work by analyzing a broader set of specific brand communication cues and estimating their moderating effects on ad spending-to-sales impact in a dynamic model.
